page: Amazon Web Services g6.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. For accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 900 GB local NVMe SSD, and network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights strong performance in specific workloads, particularly in LLM inference. The instance achieves top-tier results for prompt processing and text generation using small and medium models, with prompt processing reaching 12203.0 tokens per second for the 135M model and 2190.32 tokens per second for the 7B model. It also demonstrates strong performance in single-core CPU stress tests, image processing, and CPU encryption tests. General multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the g6.8xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile that pairs dedicated AMD EPYC CPU resources with an Ada Lovelace architecture GPU. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require GPU acceleration without the overhead of multi-GPU systems. The instance is highly suited for large language model inference, image processing, and graphics-intensive applications, while also supporting general-purpose tasks such as web serving and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.4 GHz clock speed, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, a 900 GB local NVMe SSD, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, alongside strong single-core CPU performance and image processing capabilities. Multi-core CPU operations and memory bandwidth perform at average levels. This hardware profile delivers cost-efficient acceleration for workloads such as LLM inference, image processing, and graphics-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.8xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for LLM inference and graphics workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU and AMD EPYC processor, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with one NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM
Includes 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Best suited for machine learning inference, image processing, and graphics workloads
